calendar.matters:
Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Dec-21-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Bailey Heaps, Esq., KEKER, VAN NEST & PETERS LLP, 415-391-5400, bheaps@keker.com, for Plaintiff, BALAJI K. SRINIVASAN
Navi Singh Dhillon, Esq., navidhillon@paulhastings.com; Ryan Dunbar, Esq., ryandunbar@paulhastings.com, PAUL HASTINGS LLP, (415) 856-7070, for Defendant
Raquel Sharp, CSR#10619, 925-384-0003, raquel@transcriptlegal.com. court Reporter

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument is adopted as modified.

DEFENDANT HASHFLOW FOUNDATION Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs

Defendant's "motion for award of attorneys' fees and costs" is denied without prejudice to refiling for these fees and costs at the arbitrations end. The parties are arbitrating a cryptocurrency dispute. As is common in arbitration agreements, theirs permits a party to "seek provisional injunctive relief" from a court "to ensure that the relief sought in the arbitration is not rendered ineffectual by interim harm." (Heaps Dec. Ex. 1 21(d).) This is particularly apt where, as here, an arbitration demand had been filed, but no arbitrator yet selected.

Plaintiff sought provisional injunctive relief from this court under the arbitration agreement. (Id.) Claiming it prevailed in those proceedings, defendant moved for fees and costs. However, the parties bound themselves to "economical" action in their arbitration agreement. (Id. at 21(a).) I do not find it economical for fees and costs to be litigated now on hundreds of filed pages, with another such deluge at the arbitration's end, when many of the same issues would no doubt be litigated.

Otay River Constructors v. San Diego Expressway (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 796 and Turner v. Schultz (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 974 are not to the contrary. The Otay trial court denied a motion to compel arbitration, made a prevailing-party determination and denied a fee award. (158 Cal.App.4th at 801.) I have done none of that. In Turner, the plaintiff, unlike here, sued in court "to forestall the arbitration of the dispute between the parties." (175 Cal.App.4th at 976.) Moreover, the Court of Appeal held that "the trial court could properly" make an early award of attorney fees, not that it must. (Id. at 983-84.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR#10619, 925-384-0003, raquel@transcriptlegal.com. court Reporter, Reported. (302/RBU)
